name: Taxonomy Mapping review for Stem Cell Rejuvenation bundle
summary: The taxonomy mapping is coherent. hDNAgr is included because the registry explicitly targets hematopoietic stem-cell aging, while withdrawn exercise, NR/pterostilbene muscle-regeneration, 15-PGDH muscle repair, MMP9 aged-bone repair, mitochondrial, and stromal/HSPC mobilization records remain excluded or boundary context so the public track does not absorb adjacent tissue-repair or niche-support claims.

findings.claim_or_issue: The hDNAgr registry is mapped to Stem Cell Rejuvenation because it explicitly targets HSC aging endpoints.
findings.why_it_matters: The intervention is relevant to the track even though it currently contributes watchlist context rather than efficacy evidence.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; keep the no-results and safety caveats attached.

findings.claim_or_issue: Adjacent trial and PubMed records are used as exclusions or boundary context rather than public support for the track.
findings.why_it_matters: This avoids blending stem-cell rejuvenation with NAD overlap, muscle repair, immune modulation, mitochondrial aging, or marrow-niche support without a specific mapping decision.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; revisit only if future results create a direct intervention claim for this track.
